title: Kim Brown
content:
Partner | Tax Group Regional Practice Lead - Vancouver
People.ContactEmail kbrown@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
Kim is a practical and solution-oriented tax lawyer helping clients manage risk and achieve strategic objectives.
Kim Brown* is a Partner in the Firm’s National Tax Group, Practice Lead of the firm’s Tax Group in British Columbia, and a member of our Board of Partners. Combining technical expertise with practical insight, Kim is experienced in navigating clients through complex domestic and cross-border transactions and providing actionable solutions that align with each client’s business objectives.
Kim advises multinational enterprises, pension funds, institutional investors, sovereign and Crown entities and private equity sponsors on the tax aspects of complex cross-border structures, strategic acquisitions and divestitures, capital deployment, and ongoing operations. She brings deep experience across a broad range of industries, including mining, energy, infrastructure, real estate, retail, technology and communications.
Kim is an active contributor to tax scholarship and thought leadership.  She has published extensively with leading tax publications, including the Canadian Tax Foundation, the Tax Management International Journal, and she is the co-author of Canadian Taxation of Trusts. She frequently presents on emerging tax policy and cross-border developments, serves on the Council of the International Fiscal Association (Canadian Branch) and is a Governor of the Canadian Tax Foundation.
Kim’s representative work includes advising on many of Canada’s most significant corporate and capital markets transactions, including Enbridge Inc.’s C$4.6 billion bought-deal public equity financing, RB Global’s US$7.3-billion acquisition of IAA Inc., multiple high-value mining combinations, and major U.S. and Canadian capital markets offerings by leading public issuers.
Kim earned her JD from the University of Toronto and her BComm with Distinction from the University of Victoria. She completed the CICA In-Depth Tax Course, (Levels I & II) and is admitted to the bars of Ontario (2009) and British Columbia (2019).

### Canadian Taxation of Trusts, second edition
Canadian Taxation of Trusts is an essential resource for legal and tax professionals navigating the increasingly complex landscape of trust taxation in Canada. Co-authored by McCarthy Tétrault partner Kim Brown, Canadian Taxation of Trusts is widely recognized as the leading Canadian treatise on the subject. It offers practical, in-depth analysis of recent legislative, judicial, and administrative developments, including new rules for testamentary and non-resident trusts, commercial trust reforms, and CRA audit trends. Tax professionals working on matters involving trust structures will benefit from the book’s insights.
*denotes Law Corporation
